item.description:
# Codex Token Bar v0.3.1
This release polishes the first-run experience, update path, and the live dashboard details.
## Highlights
- New first-run setup guide: confirms the Codex directory, recommends launch-at-login, and lets you enable automatic update checks. - New menu bar control: open the main window, check for updates, toggle automatic update checks, toggle launch-at-login, or quit from the macOS status bar. - Launch-at-login now starts quietly after setup, keeping the floating/status token meter available without opening the full dashboard. - Account quota display now shows a retry hint when quota reading fails, so transient network/API failures are easier to recover from. - The recent 24-hour chart hover card now appears only while hovering and floats above the curve without clipping at the edges. - The activity mode selector and README screenshots were refreshed to show cache hit rate, quota lines, and the new floating panel layout. - Release packaging keeps the styled DMG background and the Sparkle update signature checks from the v0.3.0 release line.
## Update Notes
- Sparkle checks the GitHub appcast when automatic update checks are enabled, or when you choose `Codex Token Bar -> Check for Updates...`. - Updates are still user-confirmed; the app does not silently install a new version in the background. - The app remains ad-hoc signed and is not Apple notarized. Verify the SHA256 checksum before opening if macOS warns about an unidentified developer.

# Codex Token Bar v0.3.0
This release turns Codex Token Bar into a fuller local usage console while keeping the app local-first.
## Highlights
- Adds Sparkle-based in-app update checking with signed update metadata. - Adds a DMG release package, checksums, and a versioned app zip for updates. - Adds account quota tracking with 5h / 7d trend lines, local hourly heartbeat storage, and compact pace hints. - Adds cache-hit analytics by day, hour, session, and turn, including a cache hit-rate curve in the 24h chart. - Improves the floating token-rate panel with quota context, scaling, opacity, and default-on first launch behavior. - Improves the session disappearance repair flow with a guided scan / backup / repair / verify workflow and rollback list. - Reduces scroll jank by removing low-value large shadows and moving expensive controls off hot render paths.
## Notes
- The app remains ad-hoc signed and is not Apple notarized. Verify the SHA256 checksum before opening if macOS warns about an unidentified developer. - The app reads local Codex data only. It does not upload prompts, outputs, logs, or account quota data.
